title: DDAS RiskPolicy v1
description: A company's registered risk posture, as code. Authored in YAML, stored as RFC 8785 canonical JSON; the sha256 content hash is the policy's identity in every classification derivation. Band rules use the DDAS rule DSL: comparisons (fact op literal, op in == != < <= > >=), list membership (fact in list_name, or literal in fact), boolean combinations (and/or/not, parentheses), and the literal 'else' as the final catch-all band. The DSL is deliberately not Turing-complete: no loops, no function calls. Structural validity is this schema; semantic validity (monotone appetites, matrices monotone on both axes, exhaustive ordered bands, every referenced fact declared) is enforced by the policy compiler at registration.

policy_id.description: Stable slug identifying the policy across versions.

version.description: Monotonically increasing. Policy versions are immutable once activated; any change is a new version.

approved_by.description: Sign-off ceremony for the transcription of prose appetite statements into bands.

authority_ladder.description: Ordered authority tiers mapped to the company's actual org. Tier 0 is self-approval; ascending tiers are ascending authority.

roles.description: Org roles that hold this authority (resolved against the live org structure at routing time).

quorum.description: Number of approvers required, or 'majority'. Default 1.

bands.description: Ordered least→most likely, in the company's own vocabulary (e.g. Rare, Unlikely, Possible, Likely, Almost_certain).

ratings.description: Ordered least→most severe risk ratings, in the company's own vocabulary (e.g. Low, Moderate, High, Critical). Risk-matrix cells and appetite keys must use these labels.

fact_schema.description: The typed fact vocabulary the extraction layer must fill. Every fact referenced by any band rule, likelihood rule, or trigger must be declared here.

unit.description: For money facts: ISO currency the value is normalized to (via fx_snapshot).

categories.description: The company's risk taxonomy. Each category independently demands a minimum authority tier; the engine takes the max.

bands.description: Ordered least→most severe. Rules are evaluated in order; exactly one must match ('else' as the last band's rule makes the scale exhaustive).

rule.description: DDAS rule DSL expression over declared facts, or 'else'.

required_facts.description: Facts that must be FOUND (or MANUAL) for this category to score. A NOT_FOUND required fact invokes missing_info.

likelihood_rules.description: Deterministic indicator rules, evaluated in order; the highest min_band among matching rules wins, else default_band. Never LLM judgment.

risk_matrix.description: The company's registered grid: keys are impact-band names; values are arrays of ratings, one per likelihood band (least→most likely). Must be monotone along both axes.

appetite.description: The machine-readable risk appetite statement: rating → minimum authority tier. Must be monotone in rating severity.

appetite_agent_initiated.description: Optional stricter appetite column for agent-initiated actions. Each tier must be >= the default appetite's tier for the same rating.

behavior.description: escalate: score at conservative_band (unknown = breach; never lowers a tier). needs_info: classification returns INCOMPLETE and blocks routing.

escalation_triggers.description: Named exceptional conditions — the decision-table overlay. Upward-only: a trigger can raise the tier, never lower it.

rationale.description: Flows verbatim into the decision explanation.

accumulation_rule.description: The one thing max() legitimately misses: many medium risks. Counts categories, never sums scores.

default_uplift.description: Tier uplift for agent-initiated actions in categories without an appetite_agent_initiated column.

self_approve_allowed.description: Whether agent-initiated actions may ever resolve to tier 0. When false (default posture), agents never self-approve unless an action type is explicitly whitelisted.

self_approve_whitelist.description: Action types exempt from the self-approve floor.

attestation_required_facts.description: Facts that require human attestation when the initiator is an agent; unattested ones become needs-info items.

reference_lists.description: Named lists usable in rules via 'in' (sanctions_list, home_jurisdictions, approved_precedents...). Versioned with the policy.

fx_snapshot.description: Pinned FX rates for money-band comparison. Snapshotted per policy version so classifications are not time-dependent.
